turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hey B, so uh, about that new marketing push the CEO mentioned in the all-hands last week. He said we gotta double down on TikTok because 'that's where the kids are spending their money these days.' What do you think we should prioritize first?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Yeah, I remember that. CEO was super clear—'TikTok or bust,' he basically said. So, probably start with those short video campaigns he loves. But, like, our budget's kinda tight... should we cut the Instagram stuff?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hmm, good point. He did say in the Q&A, 'Instagram's for boomers, TikTok's the future.' So yeah, slashing Instagram makes sense. But what about content? He tweeted last month that we need 'raw, unfiltered vibes'—no polished ads.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Totally, that tweet was gold. 'Polished is passé,' right? So, maybe get the interns to film quick phone vids of the product in real life. Oh, tangent—did you see his email this morning about hiring more Gen Z? He said 'they get it intuitively.'

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Ha, yeah, that email was all caps at one point. 'HIRE GEN Z NOW!' So, def prioritize that. For the strategy, though, he said in the last board meeting notes—and I have the transcript—'flood TikTok with user-generated challenges.' Like, make a hashtag thing.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: User-generated, yes! He hammered that in the founder story video too: 'Let the community own it.' But uh, what if it flops? He didn't say anything about backups.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: True, he was pretty bullish—no backups mentioned. Just 'go all in or go home.' Remember his podcast interview? 'Risk is our middle name.' So, probably no Plan B. Let's sketch a challenge: #OurProductDance or something silly.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Dance challenge? He did say 'fun first, sales second' in that AMA. Perfect. But metrics—he wants 'viral coefficient over 1.5' from his Slack rant last Friday. How do we track that without fancy tools?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Slack rant, yeah, he was fired up: 'Virality or die.' We can use TikTok analytics for shares and duets, I guess. He also said 'don't overthink tools, just ship.' So, basic dashboard.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Ship fast, got it. Oh, wait—budget again. He approved 50k for influencers in the memo, but only 'nano-influencers under 10k followers.' No big names.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Exactly, memo was clear: 'Big influencers are fake, nanos are real.' So, scout those. Timeline? He said 'launch in two weeks' in the all-hands. Aggressive, but okay.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Two weeks? Yikes. But yeah, 'speed beats perfection'—that's his mantra from the wall poster. Let's assign: I'll handle influencer outreach, you do content briefs?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Deal. And loop in the CEO on the plan—he loves weekly updates, as he said in the last one-on-one template.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Smart. Alright, this feels solid—all straight from the boss man's playbook.

input.objective: Strategy discussion on pivoting marketing to TikTok, justified by CEO/founder's statements
